response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with strong web, backend, and low-latency engineering skills, but his background has virtually no overlap with the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, and deep neural network workload optimization—none of which appear in his resume. His current AI-adjacent work (MCP frameworks, agentic tooling) is at the application/tooling layer, not hardware or model optimization. His low-latency DLang work at Sociomantic is the closest proxy to performance engineering, but it targets network I/O, not silicon or ML workloads.
strengths: Demonstrated performance engineering instincts in high-throughput, low-latency networked systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic)
Broad systems-level exposure including embedded, robotics, and C/C++ work, suggesting adaptability across abstraction layers
Current role involves AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflow tooling, indicating recent engagement with the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience—the single most critical technical requirement of the role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator workload analysis, which are the core preferred qualifications
No deep learning training or inference optimization experience; AI exposure is limited to application-layer tooling, not model performance or hardware co-design

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU expertise
